ocr.text: The first table presents data on pesticide application effects, categorized by type and survey method, with the headers indicating
the type of pesticide application, telephone survey, and web survey, under the sub-headers Effect and ICC. For outdoor sprays,
the telephone survey reports "No variation observed" with an ICC of 0.16, while the web survey indicates a "Decreasing across
quarters" effect with an ICC of 0.30. Indoor sprays show "No variation observed" in the telephone survey with an ICC of 0.05
and "No variation observed" in the web survey with an ICC of 0.20. The behind-the-neck treatment on pets reports "No
variation observed" in the telephone survey with an ICC of 0.45, contrasted by a "Decreasing across quarters" effect in the web
survey with an ICC of 0.66. Professional application in the yard has no reported variation in the telephone survey, with no ICC
data, but the web survey shows "No variation observed" with an ICC of 0.77.
Type of pesticide application  Telephone survey  Web survey
Effect  ICC  Effect  ICC
Outdoor sprays  No variation observed  0.16  Decreasing across quarters  0.30
warm season > cool season
Indoor sprays  No variation observed  0.05  No variation observed  0.20
Behind-the-neck treatment on pet  No variation observed  0.45  Decreasing across quarters  0.66
Professional application in the yard  —  —  No variation observed  0.77
Receptor  Regulation  Pathway  Function  Reference
PAR-1  CCL2  PKC/c-SRC/EGFR/MEK/ERK/AP-1  Monocytes, memory T cells, and dendritic cells activation  [47, 48]
PAR-1/-2  IL-1, TNF-α  p38/p42/44  MMP expression  [65]
PAR-1/-3  HO-1  PKCδ/c-SRC/Nrf-2  Synovial fibroblasts inflammatory  [70–72]
PAR-1/-3  MMP-13  PKCδ/c-SRC/EGFR/pI3K/AKT/AP-1  Cartilage destruction  [59, 60]
PAR-1  COX-2  ?  IL-6, CTGF, FGF1/2, VEGF, Tnc expression  [51–54]
The second table details receptors, regulations, pathways, functions, and references, with PAR-1 regulated by CCL2, activating
monocytes, memory T cells, and dendritic cells via the PKC/c-SRC/EGFR/MEK/ERK/AP-1 pathway, referenced in [47, 48].
PAR-1/-2 is regulated by IL-1 and TNF-α affecting MMP expression through the p38/p42/44 pathway, cited in [65]. PAR-1/-3
with HO-1 regulates synovial fibroblast inflammation, referenced in [70–72], while MMP-13 involves cartilage destruction via
the PKCδ/c-SRC/EGFR/pI3K/AKT/AP-1 pathway, from [59, 60]. Lastly, PAR-1 also regulates COX-2 with IL-6, CTGF,
FGF1/2, VEGF, Tnc expression via an unspecified pathway as noted in [51–54].
